rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON VS. ALBERT H WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT ALBERT WONG, JOYCE WONG DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is continued to November 9, 2020 for Plaintiff to provide supplemental briefing addressing any authority standing for the proposition that "by virtue of possession of the Note, all interest in both the Note and the corresponding deed of trust has been transferred to Plaintiff." Plaintiff shall further identify in the brief the date of purported transfer of the interest in the Deed of Trust referenced in paragraph 12 of the Complaint. (NOTE: Plaintiff shall provide information regarding transfer, not regarding the recording of such assignment. Complaint is clear that recording never occurred). If such date is corresponding with the date of Note transfer (April 5, 2007 per Ex. 2 to Complaint) Plaintiff shall also cite authority for the proposition that the "the time to seek this equitable relief would not expire as long as the deed of trust is still enforceable." (Opposition 3:14-16). Finally, Plaintiff shall address authorities supporting propriety of naming moving defendants as a party to the equitable assignment cause of action in light of Plaintiff's argument in opposition at 3:1-5. Brief shall not exceed 3 pages; shall only address the issues listed above; and shall not include argument beyond a brief explanation of why authorities stand for the propositions for which they are cited. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAM CHOY VS. JACKIE CHANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2020 line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT SAM CHOY, YI CHANG DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. This cross-complaint purports to allege 4 causes of action, all of which arise out the claim that Cross-Complaint has intertest in the Subject Property. Central to Cross-Complaint's claims is the allegation in paragraph 23 of the Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Her title claims arise out of (1) claims to the down payment amounts obtained from Family Residence prior to 2005; and/or (2) purported mortgage and tax payment contributions towards the Subject property since May 2005. In the demurrer moving party raised the statue of limitations issue, which is addressed in opposition in a 2-sentese statement at 2:12-13. This opposition does not address causes of action 1-3 and purports to reference "recent events." The down payment issue arose prior to 2005, no explanation is provided why pre-2005 events are recent. Furthermore, Cross-Complainant seeks to quiet title as of April 14, 2004. No "recent events" could contribute to the determinations regarding the statue of the title on April 14, 2004. Finally, opposition cites no authority as to when the statute of limitations starts and expires if partition/accounting claim is based on contributions towards the property dating back to 2005. Lack of opposition on these issues concedes the point. =(501/CFH)
